title: Aureline command-boundary marker, input-request, and paste-safety packet record
description: Boundary schema for the three live-session packet families that ride alongside a terminal_session_record from schemas/execution/terminal_session.schema.json: command-boundary markers (where one command begins and ends), input-requests (typed prompts for paste / secret-mode entry / interactive line-class entry under timeout), and paste-safety packets (typed review of host-initiated paste and OSC-52 round-trips before the bytes commit). The contract makes high-risk paste, remote clipboard bridging, secret-mode prompts, and command-boundary confidence reviewable rather than implicit. Vocabularies compose with - and never re-mint - the shell-integration signal kinds, the clipboard write / suppression vocabulary, and the denial-reason set frozen in schemas/terminal/session_restore_metadata.schema.json (the terminal-protocol seed). They also compose with the high-risk preview classes from the shell-interaction-safety contract, the secret-broker credential-handle vocabulary, the workspace-trust state, the admin-policy epoch, the command-dispatch descriptor, and the redaction class. Raw PTY bytes, raw escape-sequence payloads, raw paste-buffer bytes, raw clipboard bytes, raw secret values, raw command lines, and raw URLs MUST NOT appear in any record; records carry refs and counts only. Adding a new enum member, a new optional field, or a new sub-record kind is additive-minor with a command_boundary_marker_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing member is breaking and requires a new decision row.

command_boundary_marker_schema_version.description: Document-level schema version. Bumped on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, and safe in support / evidence / replay exports.

opaque_ref.description: Opaque ref to another record (terminal session, shell-integration signal, command-dispatch descriptor, credential-handle class, high-risk preview class, target-identity witness, approval-ticket envelope, etc.).

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

boundary_confidence_class.description: Confidence with which the host believes the marked region is one logical command. boundary_confirmed_by_shell_integration_signal_pair requires both command_begin and command_end signals from the shell-integration contract. The other classes are typed degraded postures; renderers MUST narrow command-boundary navigation, exit-status badges, and rerun affordances to the matching chip rather than implying confirmed boundaries.

boundary_host_scope_class.description: Host-scope projection of the host_scope_badge_class on the parent terminal-session record at the moment the boundary was marked. Renderers MUST use this row, not the live session badge, when attributing where a historical command actually executed.

command_line_class.description: Coarse class of what was entered on the line. Closed vocabulary; the exact command bytes never project.

exit_status_class.description: Closed exit-status confidence class. exit_status_unobserved_no_shell_integration is the honest answer when the shell did not emit a command_end signal.

input_request_kind_class.description: Closed vocabulary of typed prompt kinds the host opens against the user on behalf of a running shell or command-dispatch action. interactive_secret_mode_no_echo_request and interactive_password_mode_managed_echo_mask MUST suppress local echo and MUST NOT include the entered bytes in any transcript or audit body.

secret_mode_class.description: Whether the input request is in secret mode. Any secret-mode value MUST forbid transcript capture of the entered bytes; the only artefacts that may project are the input_request_id, the secret_mode_class, and the typed result class.

input_request_result_class.description: Closed result vocabulary. result_user_submitted_secret_no_summary forbids summarising the entered bytes anywhere; only the input-request id and the typed result class project.

paste_origin_class.description: Closed vocabulary of where the paste payload originated. remote_originated_paste_via_clipboard_bridge and shell_originated_osc52_round_trip_paste MUST cite a target-identity witness ref.

paste_payload_line_class.description: Coarse line / payload class. Multi-line-with-executable-intent and payload_intersects_credential_handle_class trigger paste-then-run review and high-risk preview gating; the exact bytes never project.

paste_safety_decision_class.description: Closed vocabulary of paste-safety decisions. Every refusal fails closed and cites a denial-reason class on the record.

denial_reason_class.description: Closed denial-reason vocabulary for command-boundary, input-request, and paste-safety refusals.

audit_event_id.description: Closed audit-event id vocabulary. Raw bodies, raw command lines, raw paste bytes, and raw URLs MUST NOT appear on any event.

terminal_session_record_ref.description: Ref to the terminal_session_record this marker rides under.

command_dispatch_descriptor_ref.description: Ref to the command-dispatch descriptor that admitted this command (when the host can attribute one). Required when boundary_confidence_class is boundary_inferred_from_command_dispatch_only_no_shell_signal.

command_begin_signal_ref.description: Ref to a shell_integration_signal_record with signal_kind = command_begin. Required when boundary_confidence_class is boundary_confirmed_by_shell_integration_signal_pair.

command_end_signal_ref.description: Ref to a shell_integration_signal_record with signal_kind = command_end. Required when boundary_confidence_class is boundary_confirmed_by_shell_integration_signal_pair.

exit_status_value.description: Exit status value when exit_status_class is exited_zero_success or exited_nonzero_failure or killed_by_signal. Null otherwise. The signal-name string for killed_by_signal MUST NOT project here; consumers resolve it through the command_end signal payload ref.

duration_monotonic_ms.description: Monotonic duration from command_begin to command_end in milliseconds. Null when boundary_confidence_class is anything other than boundary_confirmed_by_shell_integration_signal_pair.

transcript_line_range_class.description: Honest report of how the marked transcript region sits relative to the parent session's scrollback bound. The exact line numbers never project.

allOf.description: Confirmed shell-integration boundaries MUST cite both command_begin and command_end signal refs.
boundary_inferred_from_command_dispatch_only_no_shell_signal MUST cite a command-dispatch descriptor ref.
exited_zero_success / exited_nonzero_failure MUST cite a non-null exit_status_value.

command_boundary_marker_ref.description: Ref to the command-boundary marker the prompt is associated with, when the host can attribute one.

credential_handle_class_refs.description: Refs to credential-handle classes from the secret-broker boundary that this prompt may write into. Non-empty when secret_mode_class is one of the secret_mode_* values.

expires_at.description: Monotonic timestamp at which the prompt's authority expires. The host MUST close the prompt with result_timed_out_no_value_provided when this deadline passes; reopening requires a new input_request_record.

result_summary_class.description: Closed summary class. Secret-mode prompts MUST resolve to no_summary_secret_or_redacted; only opaque ids and the typed result class project.

closed_at.description: Monotonic timestamp at which the prompt closed. Null when result_class is result_pending_no_decision_yet.

allOf.description: Secret-mode prompts MUST cite at least one credential-handle class ref and MUST resolve result_summary_class to no_summary_secret_or_redacted.
result_pending_no_decision_yet MUST null closed_at; every other result MUST cite a non-null closed_at.

paste_payload_line_count_estimate.description: Coarse line-count estimate. Null when paste_payload_line_class is single_line_no_newline / single_line_trailing_newline / very_large_payload_above_review_threshold or when redaction-class forbids surfacing a count.

paste_payload_bytes_estimate.description: Coarse byte-count estimate. Null when redaction-class forbids surfacing a count.

high_risk_preview_class_refs.description: Refs to high-risk preview classes from the shell-interaction-safety contract that apply (multiline_terminal_paste, paste_then_run, remote_clipboard_bridge, secret_access, bidi_or_invisible_formatting_reveal, confusable_identifier_reveal, rich_active_content_render). Non-empty when paste_payload_line_class intersects an executable-intent or high-risk row.

credential_handle_class_refs.description: Refs to credential-handle classes intersected by the payload. Non-empty when paste_payload_line_class is payload_intersects_credential_handle_class.

target_identity_witness_ref.description: Remote-agent target-identity witness ref. Required when paste_origin_class is remote_originated_paste_via_clipboard_bridge or shell_originated_osc52_round_trip_paste.

preview_record_ref.description: Ref to the preview_apply_revert_record that gated the admission. Required when paste_safety_decision_class is paste_admitted_after_preview_no_run_until_user_confirms or paste_admitted_after_preview_run_intent_explicit.

run_intent_command_dispatch_descriptor_r.description: Ref to the command-dispatch descriptor admitted to run the pasted bytes. Required ONLY when paste_safety_decision_class is paste_admitted_after_preview_run_intent_explicit; the descriptor binds the run intent through the command-dispatch boundary rather than letting a paste mint authority by itself.

expires_at.description: Monotonic deadline by which a held packet MUST resolve to admitted or denied; null on already-resolved packets.

allOf.description: Remote / OSC-52 paste origins MUST cite a target-identity witness ref.
Admitted paste decisions MUST cite a preview record ref.
Explicit run-intent decisions MUST cite a command-dispatch descriptor ref so the run is attributed through the command-dispatch boundary, not through the paste itself.
Held / denied packets MUST forbid run-intent attribution.
Credential-class payloads MUST cite at least one credential-handle class ref and MUST NOT admit paste.
High-risk preview-class payloads MUST cite at least one preview-class ref.
Held / denied packets cite a typed denial-reason class.
